Title: Odoo on Azure — Release Quality, Testing, and UAT Readiness
Description: End-to-end release quality, automated testing, UAT execution, and go-live readiness track for Odoo 18 CE on Azure Container Apps. Covers governance, validation, integration testing, non-functional readiness, UAT scenarios, formal sign-off, cutover, and staging parity.
Acceptance Criteria: All 8 child issues closed with evidence artifacts in docs/evidence/. Go/no-go gate passed. Production smoke green.

Title: Release quality governance for Odoo on Azure
Parent: Odoo on Azure — Release Quality, Testing, and UAT Readiness
Description: Define severity/priority matrix, blocker policy, sign-off owners, go/no-go rules, and risk acceptance format for the Odoo-on-Azure release.
Acceptance Criteria:
Specific: Severity/priority matrix (S1-S4 x P1-P4) documented and approved by product owner.
Measurable: 100% of release roles (sign-off owners, blocker arbitrator, risk acceptor) assigned by name.
Achievable: Matrix and policy derived from existing IPAI governance templates.
Relevant: Directly gates go/no-go decision — no release without this artifact.
Time-bound: Completed within 3 business days of issue creation.

Title: Draft severity/priority matrix (S1-S4 x P1-P4)
Parent: Release quality governance for Odoo on Azure
Description: Create the 4x4 severity/priority classification grid with clear definitions and examples for each cell.
Acceptance Criteria: Matrix document exists at docs/governance/severity-priority-matrix.md with all 16 cells defined.

Title: Define blocker policy and escalation path
Parent: Release quality governance for Odoo on Azure
Description: Document what constitutes a release blocker, who can declare one, and the escalation/resolution SLA.
Acceptance Criteria: Blocker policy published with named escalation contacts and SLA per severity level.

Title: Assign sign-off owners per tower
Parent: Release quality governance for Odoo on Azure
Description: Map each functional tower (Finance, HR, Sales, Inventory, etc.) to a named sign-off owner.
Acceptance Criteria: Sign-off roster with tower, owner name, and backup documented.

Title: Define go/no-go decision rules
Parent: Release quality governance for Odoo on Azure
Description: Create the go/no-go checklist with mandatory pass criteria and acceptable risk thresholds.
Acceptance Criteria: Go/no-go checklist with pass/fail criteria and quorum rules published.

Title: Create risk acceptance format and log template
Parent: Release quality governance for Odoo on Azure
Description: Design a risk acceptance form (risk ID, description, impact, mitigation, acceptor, expiry).
Acceptance Criteria: Risk acceptance template and empty log created at docs/governance/risk-acceptance-log.md.

Title: Automated validation baseline for release candidates
Parent: Odoo on Azure — Release Quality, Testing, and UAT Readiness
Description: Establish static/contract validation, unit/module tests, staging smoke pack, regression subset, and evidence output format for automated release candidate validation.
Acceptance Criteria:
Specific: CI pipeline runs static analysis, contract tests, unit tests, smoke tests, and regression subset on every RC tag.
Measurable: >= 90% module test pass rate; 100% smoke tests green; evidence artifacts auto-generated per run.
Achievable: Builds on existing repo_health.sh, spec_validate.sh, and ci_local.sh scripts.
Relevant: Prevents regressions from reaching staging or production.
Time-bound: Pipeline operational within 5 business days of issue creation.

Title: Configure static analysis gates (linting, manifest validation)
Parent: Automated validation baseline for release candidates
Description: Add pre-commit and CI steps for flake8, pylint-odoo, and manifest schema validation on all ipai_* modules.
Acceptance Criteria: CI rejects PRs with lint errors; manifest validation covers all addons/.

Title: Build unit/module test suite baseline
Parent: Automated validation baseline for release candidates
Description: Identify and document minimum test coverage per module; ensure all existing tests run in CI.
Acceptance Criteria: Test inventory document lists modules, test count, and current pass rate.

Title: Create staging smoke test pack
Parent: Automated validation baseline for release candidates
Description: Write smoke tests that validate login, menu loading, key workflows (sale order, invoice, inventory move) on staging.
Acceptance Criteria: Smoke pack runs end-to-end in < 10 minutes; results logged to docs/evidence/.

Title: Define regression subset and rotation policy
Parent: Automated validation baseline for release candidates
Description: Select critical-path regression tests to run on every RC; rotate full suite weekly.
Acceptance Criteria: Regression subset documented; CI config references subset by default and full suite on schedule.

Title: Standardize evidence output format
Parent: Automated validation baseline for release candidates
Description: Define JSON/Markdown evidence schema: timestamp, test name, result, duration, artifact path.
Acceptance Criteria: Evidence schema documented; all test runners output conforming artifacts.

Title: Integration and data readiness for Odoo on Azure
Parent: Odoo on Azure — Release Quality, Testing, and UAT Readiness
Description: Cross-module handoff testing, UAT data pack creation, migration rehearsal, reconciliation report, and data defect tracking for Odoo-on-Azure integration readiness.
Acceptance Criteria:
Specific: Cross-module integration tests cover all inter-tower handoffs (e.g., SO to invoice, PO to inventory).
Measurable: UAT data pack contains >= 50 representative records per module; migration rehearsal completed with < 0.1% data loss.
Achievable: Uses existing demo data plus anonymized production samples.
Relevant: Data and integration failures are top go-live risks.
Time-bound: Data pack and first migration rehearsal complete within 7 business days.

Title: Map cross-module handoff test scenarios
Parent: Integration and data readiness for Odoo on Azure
Description: Document all inter-module data flows (SO->Invoice, PO->Receipt, etc.) and create test cases for each.
Acceptance Criteria: Handoff map with >= 10 cross-module flows documented and test scripts created.

Title: Build UAT data pack with representative records
Parent: Integration and data readiness for Odoo on Azure
Description: Create a seeded dataset with realistic customers, products, orders, invoices, and inventory for UAT.
Acceptance Criteria: Data pack script generates consistent dataset; docs/boards/uat-data-pack-manifest.md lists record counts.

Title: Execute migration rehearsal on staging
Parent: Integration and data readiness for Odoo on Azure
Description: Run full data migration from source to staging Odoo; measure completeness and duration.
Acceptance Criteria: Migration rehearsal log with row counts, duration, and error count < 0.1% of total records.

Title: Produce reconciliation report template
Parent: Integration and data readiness for Odoo on Azure
Description: Create a reconciliation report comparing source and target record counts, checksums, and key field values.
Acceptance Criteria: Reconciliation template exists; first rehearsal report generated and reviewed.

Title: Establish data defect tracking workflow
Parent: Integration and data readiness for Odoo on Azure
Description: Define how data defects are logged, triaged, and resolved during migration and UAT.
Acceptance Criteria: Data defect tag exists in issue tracker; triage SLA defined (P1 < 4h, P2 < 1d).

Title: Non-functional readiness validation for production approval
Parent: Odoo on Azure — Release Quality, Testing, and UAT Readiness
Description: Role/access validation, backup restore rehearsal, rollback rehearsal, and performance validation for production readiness.
Acceptance Criteria:
Specific: RBAC matrix validated against Odoo role assignments; restore and rollback rehearsals executed successfully.
Measurable: Restore RTO < 30 minutes; rollback completes in < 15 minutes; response time p95 < 2s under expected load.
Achievable: Uses Azure Container Apps scaling and Azure managed PostgreSQL backup features.
Relevant: Non-functional failures cause outages and data loss post-go-live.
Time-bound: All rehearsals complete within 5 business days before go-live.

Title: Validate RBAC matrix against Odoo role assignments
Parent: Non-functional readiness validation for production approval
Description: Audit all Odoo user groups and access rights against the approved RBAC matrix.
Acceptance Criteria: RBAC audit report shows 100% alignment between matrix and Odoo config; deviations logged as issues.

Title: Execute backup restore rehearsal
Parent: Non-functional readiness validation for production approval
Description: Restore a production-like backup to a clean environment and verify data integrity.
Acceptance Criteria: Restore completed in < 30 minutes; spot-check of 20 records shows 100% integrity.

Title: Execute rollback rehearsal
Parent: Non-functional readiness validation for production approval
Description: Simulate a failed deployment and roll back to previous container revision and database snapshot.
Acceptance Criteria: Rollback completed in < 15 minutes; application functional post-rollback; evidence logged.

Title: Run performance validation under expected load
Parent: Non-functional readiness validation for production approval
Description: Load test staging with expected concurrent users; measure response times, error rates, and resource usage.
Acceptance Criteria: p95 response time < 2s; error rate < 1%; CPU/memory within ACA scaling limits.

Title: Document non-functional readiness evidence pack
Parent: Non-functional readiness validation for production approval
Description: Compile all non-functional test results into a single evidence pack for go/no-go review.
Acceptance Criteria: Evidence pack at docs/evidence/<date>/non-functional/ with all 4 rehearsal reports.

Title: UAT scenario pack baseline and ownership
Parent: Odoo on Azure — Release Quality, Testing, and UAT Readiness
Description: Create uat-scenarios.md with scenario coverage for all functional towers, named owners, and execution templates.
Acceptance Criteria:
Specific: uat-scenarios.md contains numbered scenarios grouped by tower with named owner per scenario.
Measurable: >= 5 scenarios per tower; 100% of scenarios have a named owner and expected result.
Achievable: Derived from existing functional specs and cross-module handoff map.
Relevant: UAT cannot start without a baselined scenario pack.
Time-bound: Scenario pack baselined within 4 business days of issue creation.

Title: Create uat-scenarios.md template with tower sections
Parent: UAT scenario pack baseline and ownership
Description: Set up the scenario document structure with sections for each functional tower.
Acceptance Criteria: Template exists at docs/boards/uat-scenarios.md with all tower sections and scenario numbering.

Title: Write Finance tower UAT scenarios
Parent: UAT scenario pack baseline and ownership
Description: Author >= 5 UAT scenarios covering invoicing, payments, reconciliation, and reporting.
Acceptance Criteria: Finance section has >= 5 scenarios with steps, expected results, and named owner.

Title: Write Sales and CRM tower UAT scenarios
Parent: UAT scenario pack baseline and ownership
Description: Author >= 5 UAT scenarios covering lead-to-order, quotations, and pipeline reporting.
Acceptance Criteria: Sales/CRM section has >= 5 scenarios with steps, expected results, and named owner.

Title: Write Inventory and Purchasing tower UAT scenarios
Parent: UAT scenario pack baseline and ownership
Description: Author >= 5 UAT scenarios covering PO flow, receipts, stock moves, and reorder rules.
Acceptance Criteria: Inventory/Purchasing section has >= 5 scenarios with steps, expected results, and named owner.

Title: Write HR and Payroll tower UAT scenarios
Parent: UAT scenario pack baseline and ownership
Description: Author >= 5 UAT scenarios covering employee onboarding, leave, attendance, and payroll run.
Acceptance Criteria: HR/Payroll section has >= 5 scenarios with steps, expected results, and named owner.

Title: Assign scenario owners and get baseline sign-off
Parent: UAT scenario pack baseline and ownership
Description: Review all scenarios with tower leads; assign owners; get written confirmation of baseline.
Acceptance Criteria: All scenarios have named owners; baseline approval recorded in uat-scenarios.md header.

Title: Formal UAT execution and business sign-off
Parent: Odoo on Azure — Release Quality, Testing, and UAT Readiness
Description: Execute 4 UAT waves with daily triage, 100% mandatory scenario completion, tower sign-offs, and a consolidated UAT summary report.
Acceptance Criteria:
Specific: 4 UAT waves executed (core, extended, edge-case, regression); daily defect triage held.
Measurable: 100% of mandatory scenarios executed; all S1/S2 defects resolved before sign-off; tower sign-off collected from each owner.
Achievable: Builds on baselined scenario pack and UAT data pack.
Relevant: Business sign-off is a hard gate for go-live.
Time-bound: UAT execution completes within 10 business days; sign-off within 2 days of UAT close.

Title: Execute UAT Wave 1 — Core workflows
Parent: Formal UAT execution and business sign-off
Description: Run all mandatory core workflow scenarios across every tower.
Acceptance Criteria: Wave 1 results logged; pass/fail per scenario recorded; S1/S2 defects filed.

Title: Execute UAT Wave 2 — Extended and cross-module scenarios
Parent: Formal UAT execution and business sign-off
Description: Run extended scenarios including cross-module handoffs and less common paths.
Acceptance Criteria: Wave 2 results logged; cumulative pass rate tracked.

Title: Execute UAT Wave 3 — Edge cases and negative testing
Parent: Formal UAT execution and business sign-off
Description: Run edge-case and negative-path scenarios (invalid data, permission denied, concurrent edits).
Acceptance Criteria: Wave 3 results logged; edge-case defects categorized and triaged.

Title: Execute UAT Wave 4 — Regression after defect fixes
Parent: Formal UAT execution and business sign-off
Description: Re-run failed scenarios from Waves 1-3 plus regression subset after defect fixes are deployed.
Acceptance Criteria: Wave 4 results show 100% mandatory pass; no new S1/S2 regressions.

Title: Run daily defect triage during UAT
Parent: Formal UAT execution and business sign-off
Description: Hold daily 15-minute triage to classify, assign, and prioritize UAT defects.
Acceptance Criteria: Triage log with daily entries; all S1 defects assigned within 4 hours.

Title: Collect tower sign-offs and produce consolidated UAT summary
Parent: Formal UAT execution and business sign-off
Description: Get written sign-off from each tower owner; compile into a single UAT summary report.
Acceptance Criteria: Consolidated UAT summary at docs/evidence/<date>/uat/uat-summary.md with all tower sign-offs attached.

Title: Go-live readiness, cutover control, and hypercare
Parent: Odoo on Azure — Release Quality, Testing, and UAT Readiness
Description: Assemble release evidence pack, run go/no-go review, execute production smoke tests, define hypercare ownership and rotation, and establish exit criteria.
Acceptance Criteria:
Specific: Release evidence pack assembled; go/no-go review conducted with all stakeholders; production smoke tests pass.
Measurable: 100% of go/no-go checklist items resolved; production smoke green within 30 minutes of deploy; hypercare roster covers 7x24 for first 5 days.
Achievable: All prerequisite issues (governance, testing, UAT, non-functional) are closed before this issue starts.
Relevant: This is the final gate — no production traffic without passing this issue.
Time-bound: Go/no-go decision within 1 business day of UAT sign-off; hypercare runs for 5 business days post-go-live.

Title: Assemble release evidence pack
Parent: Go-live readiness, cutover control, and hypercare
Description: Compile all testing evidence, UAT sign-offs, non-functional results, and risk acceptances into a single pack.
Acceptance Criteria: Evidence pack directory at docs/evidence/<date>/release/ contains all required artifacts.

Title: Conduct go/no-go review meeting
Parent: Go-live readiness, cutover control, and hypercare
Description: Present evidence pack to stakeholders; walk through go/no-go checklist; record decision.
Acceptance Criteria: Go/no-go decision recorded with rationale; blocker list empty or risks accepted.

Title: Execute production smoke tests post-deploy
Parent: Go-live readiness, cutover control, and hypercare
Description: Run smoke test pack against production within 30 minutes of deployment.
Acceptance Criteria: Production smoke results: 100% pass; evidence logged with timestamps.

Title: Define hypercare roster and escalation matrix
Parent: Go-live readiness, cutover control, and hypercare
Description: Create 7-day hypercare rotation with named on-call, escalation paths, and handoff times.
Acceptance Criteria: Hypercare roster published with daily slots filled and escalation contacts listed.

Title: Define hypercare exit criteria
Parent: Go-live readiness, cutover control, and hypercare
Description: Document measurable criteria for exiting hypercare (e.g., < 2 P1s in 48h, all P1s resolved).
Acceptance Criteria: Exit criteria documented and approved; daily hypercare status tracked against criteria.

Title: Staging parity and release-candidate immutability
Parent: Odoo on Azure — Release Quality, Testing, and UAT Readiness
Description: Establish staging environment parity policy, drift reporting, RC freeze rules, and approved-defect-fix-only deployment policy.
Acceptance Criteria:
Specific: Staging environment matches production config (ACA revision, env vars, DB schema) with documented exceptions only.
Measurable: Drift report runs daily; zero unapproved divergences at RC freeze; only approved defect-fix PRs merged after freeze.
Achievable: Uses Azure CLI diff and GitHub branch protection rules.
Relevant: Staging drift is a top cause of go-live surprises.
Time-bound: Parity policy enforced within 3 business days; drift reporting automated within 5 business days.

Title: Document staging parity policy
Parent: Staging parity and release-candidate immutability
Description: Write policy defining what must match between staging and production (container image, env vars, DB schema, addons list).
Acceptance Criteria: Policy at docs/governance/staging-parity-policy.md with enumerated parity dimensions.

Title: Automate daily staging drift report
Parent: Staging parity and release-candidate immutability
Description: Create a script or CI job that compares staging and production configs and reports divergences.
Acceptance Criteria: Drift report runs daily in CI; output saved to docs/evidence/; alert on divergence.

Title: Implement RC freeze branch protection
Parent: Staging parity and release-candidate immutability
Description: Configure GitHub branch protection on release branches to require approval and status checks after RC tag.
Acceptance Criteria: Branch protection rule active; unapproved PRs blocked; evidence via gh api.

Title: Define approved-defect-fix-only merge policy
Parent: Staging parity and release-candidate immutability
Description: Document and enforce that only PRs linked to approved defect issues can merge after RC freeze.
Acceptance Criteria: Policy documented; PR template includes defect-link field; CI validates linkage.
